tracks.purpose: Repo-grounded prompt/agent evals for planning, honesty, proof claims, and project-state reporting.

sourceMix.description: Derived from issues and corrections already visible in repo history, reports, or contract tests.
Derived from README, docs, backlog, and verification artifacts rather than synthetic benchmarks.
Used sparingly where the repo needs stronger anti-overclaim behavior than logs alone can provide.

notes: No raw production logs are checked into this repo today.
Until sanitized traces exist, historical regressions and repo-grounded maintainer tasks are the safest production-like proxy.

tracks.purpose: Production-like runtime scenarios for routing, leases, compatibility constraints, adapter safety, and release gating.

sourceMix.description: Built from current client surface metadata, server policy modeling, and real project failure modes already known in the repo.

notes: Runtime lab cases intentionally overrepresent edge cases because the planner already handles happy-path routing better than adversarial runtime correctness.

dailyOrPerChange: Run repo contract tests and npm tests on every prompt/tool/model/config change.
Re-score seed-prompt fixtures with binary checks plus rubric scoring.
Review any covered->blocked, blocked->covered, or unsupported-claim deltas instead of only total score.
beforeRelease: Run held-out seed and runtime cases separately from the daily tuning set.
Require human calibration on all held-out outcomes.
Update verification artifacts with what was actually run on real hosts.
changeManagement: If a prompt or tool change improves one family but regresses unsupported-claim rate, the change should not be accepted silently.
Rubric changes require a documented calibration pass before historical comparisons are reused.
